document.title: MMALS G2: A Geometric Perspective on Continual Functional Memory

estimators.name: Intervention estimator (route-function alignment)
estimators.method: SVD of functional deltas under input perturbation vs. route-logit perturbation subspace; principal-angle cosine
estimators.reference_oracle: none yet -- requires controlled host-swap ground truth
estimators.notes: Candidate 1 of 3 in Section 5.1. Not yet compared against Jacobian or counterfactual-contribution estimators on a held-out qualification split.

estimators.name: Jacobian estimator (route-function alignment)
estimators.method: Dominant right-singular subspace comparison of router Jacobian and functional Jacobian
estimators.notes: Flagged in paper as computationally direct but potentially unstable and coordinate-sensitive -- expected to underperform on cross-seed stability test in Phase P3.

estimators.name: Counterfactual contribution estimator (route-function alignment)
estimators.method: Host-ablation loss delta correlated with route confidence via rank correlation
estimators.notes: Described as behaviourally grounded rather than geometrically pure. Candidate default if the geometric estimators fail cross-seed stability.

gates.name: Reproducibility
gates.criterion: Clean build, seeded runs, immutable configs, artifact hashes.
gates.consequence_if_failed: No claim above 'established' may cite this codebase as evidence.

gates.name: Diagnostic validity
gates.criterion: Route regret and functional probes recover injected failures.
gates.consequence_if_failed: Route-function alignment claims remain g2-candidate; no repair-trigger automation permitted.

gates.name: Estimator calibration
gates.criterion: Declared interval coverage and acceptable boundary bias.
gates.consequence_if_failed: Estimator demoted to advisory signal only, cannot feed a hard gate.

gates.name: Meta-calibration
gates.criterion: False admissibility and GateRegret below frozen thresholds.
gates.consequence_if_failed: Permits soft advisory gate; forbids hard-admissibility claim (Section 16.4).

gates.name: Failure separation
gates.criterion: Retention, routing, host drift, and CAL compensation are distinguishable.

gates.name: Operational gain
gates.criterion: Better utility under matched accuracy, cost, memory, and latency budgets.
gates.consequence_if_failed: Permits publication as diagnostic study only, not as operational-control specification (Section 16.4).

gates.name: Safety humility
gates.criterion: Hard claims restricted to validated scope; indeterminate and fallback paths tested.

gates.name: Cross-stream robustness
gates.criterion: Repeated on natural and engineering streams across seeds.

gates.name: Audit completeness
gates.criterion: Every decision and claim reconstructable from Chronicle and evidence manifests.

gates.name: External review readiness
gates.criterion: Geometry, CL, calibration, and systems reviewer issues closed.

phases.name: Route-function estimator study
phases.contamination_check: P3 must select the winning estimator using SPLIT-DEV-01 only. SPLIT-CALIBRATION-01 (produced in P2) and the future SPLIT-QUALIFICATION-01 (P5) must remain untouched by this selection, or R2/R3 become circular per the open review note on this manifest.

phases.name: Meta-calibration gates
phases.contamination_check: R3 thresholds (FARgate ceiling, GateRegret bound) must be frozen and recorded in gates[].threshold_value with threshold_frozen=true BEFORE this phase consumes SPLIT-QUALIFICATION-01. Currently unfrozen -- blocks P5 start.

claims.text: Geometric objects (distances, tangent directions, transport maps, admissible regions) can predict or improve continual-system behaviour under matched budgets.
claims.falsification_test: No geometric object outperforms matched-compute non-geometric baselines (replay, EWC, PNN, sparse MoE) on any tested endpoint in Phase P6/P8.

claims.text: The G1 evidence discipline (descriptive, predictive, causal, operational) is a necessary qualification ladder before geometric claims are admitted.

Route-function alignment A_r,h can be operationally estimated via intervention, Jacobian, or counterfactual-contribution methods, and a repair action requires agreement across behaviour, geometry, and stability diagnostics.
claims.falsification_test: None of the three estimators shows cross-seed stability or predicts future route regret better than a scalar confidence baseline (Phase P3 required tests).

claims.text: The admissibility projector must treat its own constraint estimates (retention, risk, cost, audit visibility) as uncertain, using LCB/UCB gating with an explicit indeterminate state rather than converting uncertainty into permission.
claims.falsification_test: Gate estimators cannot maintain preregistered coverage on a qualification split untouched by model selection (Phase P5), or FARgate/GateRegret exceed frozen thresholds once those thresholds exist.

claims.text: A hard admissibility constraint (structural projection) is only justified when its input estimators can be estimated reliably and projection does not destroy learning.
claims.falsification_test: Not applicable while deferred -- claim is explicitly out of scope until R3 and the soft-admissibility benefit in P6 pass.

claims.text: Lie group / Grassmann-manifold route structure should only be introduced if it outperforms vector prototypes under equal compute and improves causal intervention specificity.
